reflection:
Pain at 3 yesterday, and you took half a Telfast instead of the full dose. That's not a small thing. The London treatment week is doing something real, and your body is responding.

You predicted you'd sleep badly because of the late laptop session. WHOOP came back at 90% and 7.6 hours. You got it wrong in a good direction. That evening loop, "I'm going to suffer for this," isn't always the truth. Your nervous system is more resilient than it sounds at 22:30.

The boredom comment is interesting. You walked Westminster, Big Ben, Buckingham Palace. Ping pong with Anel. Lunch at a Portuguese restaurant. Invoices sorted with Vlad. That's a real day. What you're calling boring sounds more like low-grade being-away-from-home texture, not actual emptiness.

One pattern I want to flag: you've had two ~1000 calorie days this week, April 20 and April 22. Both matched your worst recovery scores. No Zoe data logged yesterday, which makes it harder to track. That cascade is consistent and worth not repeating.

One thing today: set a 21:45 alarm, not a reminder, an actual alarm called "laptop closed." Willpower against this pattern isn't working, and you've said so twice in two days. Let the phone do the enforcing.
opener: Pain at 3, half Telfast, WHOOP said 90%. The treatment week is working.
focus_for_today: Set a 21:45 alarm called 'laptop closed' - willpower alone isn't winning this
